heading: Continuous Cloud Backups
body:
Cluster owners may enable [Continuous Cloud Backup](pit-restore)
from Cloud Backups. PIT (Point in Time) backups are billed based on the disk space
occupied by an internal oplog, combined with the cloud backup
snapshot size.

You can [Configure Backup Options for the Cluster](create-cluster-backups) PIT (Point in Time) backups to
cover a window of time that you specify. Longer backup windows result
in larger oplogs and higher backup costs.

To compute the cost for Continuous Cloud Backups, Atlas obtains the raw
metric data from the cloud providers and calculates the *total* size of
all snapshots and the amount of storage used per month:

- The cloud provider

- The region in which the snapshots are stored

- The usage tier

| Cloud Provider            | 0-5 GB Storage Used (Tier 0) | 5-100 GB Storage Used (Tier 1) | 100-250 GB Storage Used (Tier 2) | 250-500 GB Storage Used (Tier 3) | >500 GB Storage Used (Tier 4) |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ | -------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| AWS (Amazon Web Services) |                              | $1.00 to $1.55                 | $0.75 to $1.20                   | $0.50 to $0.80                   | $0.25 to $0.40                |
| Azure (Microsoft Azure)   |                              | $1.00 to $3.95                 | $0.75 to $2.95                   | $0.50 to $2.00                   | $0.55 to $1.00                |
| Google Cloud              |                              | $0.60 to $0.95                 | $0.45 to $0.70                   | $0.30 to $0.50                   | $0.15 to $0.25                |

> **Example:** A cluster on AWS (Amazon Web Services) in the `US_EAST_1` region has a combined total
> snapshot and oplog size of 115 GB. The first 5 GB are free. The
> remaining 110 GB are billed at $1.00 from 5 to 100 GB, and at $0.75
> from 100 GB to 115 GB:
>
> (95 × $1.00) + (15 × $0.75) = $106.25 per month

> **Note:** Atlas applies a default backup policy to Cloud Backup-enabled
> clusters on creation. Review and [backup policy](cloud-provider-backup-schedule) as needed.
